title: Simone de Beauvoir | Books, Feminism, The Second Sex, Biography, & Facts | Britannica
text:
Section Title: Simone de Beauvoir > Where was Simone de Beauvoir educated? > Why is Simone de Beauvoir important?
Content:
**Simone de Beauvoir** (born January 9, 1908, [Paris](https://www.britannica.com/place/Paris) , France—died April 14, 1986, Paris) was a French writer and [feminist](https://www.britannica.com/event/womens-movement) , a member of the [intellectual](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/intellectual) fellowship of philosopher-writers who have given a literary transcription to the themes of [existentialism](https://www.britannica.com/topic/existentialism) . She is known primarily for her [treatise](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/treatise) *Le Deuxième Sexe,* 2 vol.

Section Title: Simone de Beauvoir > Where was Simone de Beauvoir educated? > Why is Simone de Beauvoir important?
Content:
Britannica Quiz The Literary World
She also wrote four books of philosophy, including *Pour une Morale de* *l’ambiguité* (1947; *The Ethics of Ambiguity* ); travel books on China ( *La Longue Marche: essai sur la Chine* [1957]; *The Long March* ) and the [United States](https://www.britannica.com/place/United-States) ( *L’Amérique au jour de jour* [1948]; *America Day by Day* ); and a number of essays, some of them book-length, the best known of which is *The Second Sex.* In 2009 a new English-language translation of *The Second Sex* was published, making the entire original text available to English-speaking readers for the first time; the earlier translation (1953) had been severely edited.
